_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Ear Pinna Reconstruction with costal cartilage / Prosthesis (including the cost of prosthesis / implants)
source_files: Ear Pinna Reconstruction with costal cartilage  Prosthesis_Medco.pdf
Ear Pinna Reconstruction with costal cartilage  Prosthesis_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Microtia (congenitally small and malformed external ear)
Craniofacial microsomia (malformations affecting anatomical regions from first and second branchial arches)
Child with microtia has reached sufficient age (between 3 and 6 years old) for ear reconstruction

preauth.label: Clinical notes with planned line of treatment, supporting documents if indication is non traumatic.
In case of injury then MLC/FIR report

claim.label: Detailed indoor case papers and treatment information
Clinical photograph of the affected ear
Invoice/Barcode of Prosthesis/ Implant used (if applicable)

ppd_preauth.q: Did the clinical notes and planned line of treatment/clinical photograph justify the need for surgery?

common_queries: Clinical notes or clinical photograph do not justify the need for ear reconstruction surgery.
MLC/FIR report not submitted in cases of trauma.
Supporting reports or general clinical photograph missing at pre-authorization.
Clinical photograph of the affected ear not submitted at claim stage.
Invoice or barcode of Prosthesis/Implant not submitted where applicable.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.
surgical_options: Autologous ear reconstruction (AER) using costal cartilage grafts (standard clinical practice). This involves harvesting costal cartilages, carving them into a three-dimensional ear shape, and implanting beneath the skin in the auricular region.
Costal Cartilage Fabrication Methods include reverse engineering (RE), computer-aided design (CAD), and additive manufacturing (AM).
Prosthetic and alloplastic approaches (while autologous cartilage is standard, these methods also exist).
additional_information.pre_op_preparation: In case of unilateral microtia, otolaryngologist and audiologist consultations are required. In case of bilateral atresia, diagnostic brainstem auditory-evoked responses should be performed.
additional_information.fitness_criteria: Child with microtia has reached sufficient age, which may be between 3 and 6 years old.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment, especially to a Tertiary Hospital.
